design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through linear stages defeating waves of enemies using punches, kicks, and occasional weapons (nunchaku); advance to next stage upon clearing all threats
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing close-quarters combat against multiple armed enemies while avoiding environmental hazards and weapon disarmament mechanics
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lone martial arts vigilante protecting territory and rescuing a hostage from an organized gang threat in an Americanized urban setting
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Genre-defining beat-em-up with explicit American urban gang narrative (spiritual sequel to Kung-Fu Master with Americanized setting); emphasizes 2D platform movement combined with melee combat; weapon pickup system creates risk/reward tension (holding nunchaku leaves player vulnerable to disarmament)

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives system (standard arcade convention, precise count not documented)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; no mid-stage checkpoints; death resets current stage
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; arcade-driven fixed difficulty with no documented difficulty selection or adjustment options

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon pickup system: nunchaku available as temporary power-up that enhances attack capability but increases vulnerability if player is struck while wielding it
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential stage unlock through stage completion; five stages in fixed order: street → junkyard → Brooklyn Bridge → back street → under-construction building rooftop
replayability_hooks: Arcade-style high score pursuit across multiple playthroughs
Varied enemy types with different weapons and attack patterns (knife-wielders, chain-wielders, motorbike riders, gun-wielders) requiring tactical adaptation
Multiple console ports offer platform-specific visual and audio variations

world_building_techniques: Visual stage design establishing urban New York City geography (street → industrial junkyard → Brooklyn Bridge landmark → residential back street → construction site)
Enemy faction visual identity: Skinheads with Mohawk and spiked hairdos establish gang aesthetic and cultural context
Environmental storytelling through stage progression suggesting territory expansion and final confrontation at building climax

boss_design.philosophy: Final antagonist represents faction leadership; Giant Devil functions as stage 5 climactic confrontation following player progression through enemy-held territory
notable_bosses: Giant Devil - leader of Skinheads gang; final stage boss on construction site rooftop

faction_indicators: Skinheads gang with visual markers (Mohawk/spiked hairdos) establishing organized criminal hierarchy
Gang rule under singular leader (Giant Devil) indicates structured faction organization
Territory control narrative: player defends 'turf' against gang encroachment
danger_signals: Escalating weapon presence: early stages feature melee weapons (knives, chains), later stages introduce firearms
Kidnapping as hostage-taking mechanic (Madonna/Maria) indicates organized criminal tactics
Close-quarters choking attacks suggest desperation and violence escalation when enemies close distance
environmental_storytelling: Five-stage journey through urban geography maps gang territorial spread
Junkyard stage suggests industrial criminal operations and resource control
Brooklyn Bridge as geographic checkpoint marks progression into contested territory
Under-construction building as final confrontation site: lawless space outside civilian oversight
